title: Closure may outlive the current function
explanation: This error occurs when a closure captures a reference to a local variable but might outlive the function where that variable is defined. This commonly happens when passing closures to threads or returning them from functions.
error_message: error[E0373]: closure may outlive the current function, but it borrows `name`, which is owned by the current function

fixes.description: Use the move keyword to transfer ownership
Clone before moving into the closure

tip: The `move` keyword forces the closure to take ownership of all captured variables. Use clone() if you need the value in both places.
